content: Hey, can you update the deployment detail view? Right now when I click on a deployment it expands inline in the table. I want it to be a modal instead.

Sure, I can convert the inline expansion to a modal dialog. I'll create a modal component that shows the deployment details — name, status, target, logs, and timestamps. Should it be a full-screen modal or a centered dialog?
Centered dialog, not full screen. And while you're at it, remove the draft and ready status chips from the deployment list. Only show 'Deployed' status — the intermediate states just add noise.
Got it — centered modal for details, and I'll filter the status chips to only show 'Deployed'. Should I keep the status column in the table or remove it entirely since there'd only be one value?
Keep the column but yeah just show deployed. The draft/ready states are internal implementation details that users shouldn't see.

Makes sense — implementation states shouldn't leak into the UI. I'll update the deployment list to show only 'Deployed' badges and convert the detail view to a centered modal. Let me also add a close button and click-outside-to-dismiss.

Yeah and make sure the modal has the deployment logs with auto-scroll. But the auto-scroll should respect when the user scrolls up — if they've scrolled up to read old logs, don't yank them back to the bottom.

Smart — I'll implement a 'stick to bottom' behavior that only auto-scrolls when the user is within 40px of the bottom. If they scroll up, it pauses auto-scroll until they scroll back down. Here's the implementation plan...
Perfect. Also the progress indicator for active deployments — don't show it inline in the table. It should be in the modal overlay. The table should just show the final state.

Right, so the table is a clean summary view and all the details/progress live in the modal. I'll move the progress bar and log stream into the modal and keep the table rows simple — just name, target, status, and timestamp.
Exactly. The table is the overview, the modal is the deep dive. That should be the pattern everywhere — lists are clean summaries, details go in modals.

Got it — I'll apply that pattern consistently. List views show summary data only, detail/drill-down always opens in a modal. I'll audit the other pages (targets, environments) to make sure they follow the same pattern.
Good. And one more thing — the spacing in the dashboard is too generous. Tighten it up. This is a power-user tool, not a consumer app. I want more information density.

I'll reduce padding, margins, and line-height across the dashboard. Smaller font sizes for secondary info, tighter table rows, compact card layouts. Should I keep the current spacing for the sidebar nav or tighten that too?
Tighten everything. The sidebar, the tables, the cards. I want it to feel like a professional ops dashboard, not a marketing landing page.

Understood — going for a Grafana/Datadog density level. Compact everything: 12px base font for data, 14px for headers, tight 4-8px padding on table cells, minimal card margins. Professional ops dashboard aesthetic.
Don't go that extreme on font size. 14px base is fine, just reduce the whitespace and padding. The fonts are fine as-is.

Got it — keep current font sizes, just compress the spacing. Reduce padding from 16-24px down to 8-12px, tighten table row height, minimize margins between sections. The content stays readable, just more of it visible at once.
